medium.q: What grain is the primary ingredient in injera, the Ethiopian flatbread?
What spice blend, whose name means 'head of the shop', is central to North African cooking?
Bibimbap is a Korean dish served in a hot stone bowl. What does 'bibimbap' literally translate to?
Which Japanese fermented paste made from soybeans is used as a base for miso soup?
What type of pastry dough is used to make baklava?
What is the main protein in a traditional Peruvian ceviche?
medium.a: Raw fish (typically sea bass or corvina)

medium.q: Which Indian flatbread is cooked directly on an open flame and puffs up with hot air?
What acidic ingredient 'cooks' the fish in ceviche without heat?
Garam masala is a spice blend widely used in which regional cuisine?
What is the name of the Japanese rice wine used in cooking and as a drink?
Which country's cuisine features a dish called 'bobotie', a spiced minced meat bake with egg custard topping?
What Vietnamese herb-heavy noodle salad dish is often served with peanut dipping sauce?
What is the Spanish term for small savoury bar snacks eaten throughout Spain?
Which country is home to the dish 'laksa', a spicy noodle soup in coconut broth?
What key spice gives a traditional paella its yellow colour?
Sofrito is an aromatic base of cooked vegetables used extensively in which cuisine?
What is 'natto', a traditional Japanese breakfast food?
Which East African country is famous for nyama choma, slow-roasted goat or beef?
What French cooking technique involves slow-cooking duck or pork in its own fat?
Which spice, derived from the dried stigmas of a flower, is the world's most expensive by weight?
What is the traditional South Korean fermentation vessel used to store kimchi over winter?
What is 'za'atar', widely used in Levantine cuisine?
medium.a: A herb and spice blend (thyme, sesame, sumac)

medium.q: Which grain is traditional couscous made from?
What is the main filling in a traditional Cuban 'ropa vieja'?
Which Italian city is most associated with the original Bolognese meat sauce?
What is 'harissa', used widely in Tunisian and Moroccan cooking?
Fish sauce is an essential condiment in which Southeast Asian country's cuisine?
What type of rice is traditionally used to make Japanese sushi?
medium.a: Short-grain Japanese rice (seasoned with rice vinegar)

medium.q: Which Mexican sauce is made with chillies, chocolate, and dozens of other ingredients?
What is 'injera' typically used as in Ethiopian cuisine — beyond just a food itself?
medium.a: An edible plate / scoop for other dishes

medium.q: What is the name of the spiced clarified butter used in Ethiopian cooking?
Which South American country is famous for its chimichurri sauce served with grilled meat?
What is 'dukkah', a condiment popular in Egyptian cuisine?
medium.a: A nut, seed, and spice blend

medium.q: What is the signature Indonesian fermented soybean cake used as a protein in many dishes?
What type of Japanese noodle is made from buckwheat flour?
Which country is home to the street food 'arepas de choclo', sweet corn cakes with cheese?
What is the name of the Lebanese sesame seed paste used in hummus and other dishes?
Which French region is famous for its 'bouillabaisse' fish stew?
What is the key fermented ingredient that gives a 'sourdough' its sour taste?
medium.a: Wild yeast and lactic acid bacteria (sourdough starter)

medium.q: What spice is most responsible for the orange-yellow colour of Indian curries?
What is 'elote', a popular Mexican street food?
medium.a: Grilled corn on the cob topped with mayonnaise, chilli, cheese, and lime

medium.q: Which Japanese city is most closely associated with 'tonkotsu' ramen?
What is 'sumac', used as a souring agent in Middle Eastern cooking?
medium.a: A dark red berry ground into a tangy spice

medium.q: Which Caribbean island is most associated with the dish 'ackee and saltfish'?
What is 'paneer', a key protein in many Indian vegetarian dishes?
What West African staple is made by pounding boiled plantain, cassava, or yam into a dough?
Which spice, native to the Maluku Islands, was so valuable it sparked European colonial expeditions?
What is 'bunny chow', a South African street food?
medium.a: A hollowed-out loaf of bread filled with curry

medium.q: What is the Chinese cooking technique 'wok hei'?
medium.a: The smoky, charred flavour imparted by a very hot wok

medium.q: Which Filipino dish consists of meat (often pork or chicken) braised in vinegar, soy sauce, and garlic?
What is 'piri piri', widely used in Mozambican and Portuguese cooking?
medium.a: A hot sauce made from African bird's eye chillies

medium.q: What is 'bao', a popular street food in China and Taiwan?
What is 'horchata', a popular drink in Mexican street food culture?
medium.a: A sweet rice milk drink flavoured with cinnamon

medium.q: Which Italian dessert is made from espresso poured over vanilla ice cream?
What is 'berbere', a key spice mix in Ethiopian cooking?
medium.a: A blend of chilli peppers, fenugreek, coriander, and other spices

hard.q: What is the name of the Oaxacan Mexican chocolate drink made with water and cacao paste, whipped frothy without milk?
Which Japanese knife-cutting technique creates paper-thin diagonal slices for fish or tofu?
What is 'maafe', a rich peanut stew, a staple dish in which West African country?
What is the name of the fermented fish paste that forms the backbone of Burmese cuisine?
Which Filipino dessert is a layered frozen treat made with shaved ice, sweetened red beans, coconut, and leche flan?
What is 'surstömming', famously one of the world's most pungent fermented foods?
What Peruvian pepper, deep red with smoky fruity notes, is essential to many Andean dishes?
In French cuisine, what is 'mirepoix'?
hard.a: A base of diced onion, carrot, and celery used to flavour stocks and sauces

hard.q: What is 'tepache', a fermented Mexican street drink?
hard.a: A lightly fermented drink made from pineapple rinds, piloncillo, and spices

hard.q: What is 'Joël Robuchon's' most iconic dish, a potato purée he reportedly uses nearly equal weight butter to potato in?
What Romanian bread, braided and enriched with eggs and butter, is eaten at Easter and Christmas?
What is 'kashk', a fermented dairy product used as a tangy topping in Persian and Middle Eastern cooking?
hard.a: A thick whey product (similar to dried soured whey)

hard.q: What is the Japanese term for the strict culinary etiquette governing kaiseki multi-course meal presentation?
hard.a: Ma (間) — the concept of spacing and interval in kaiseki

hard.q: Which Turkish dessert consists of shredded wheat pastry filled with white cheese and soaked in syrup?
What is 'caldo tlalpeño', a Mexican dish named after a borough of Mexico City?
hard.a: A smoky chipotle and chickpea soup with chicken

hard.q: What is the name of the Yemeni spice blend featuring black lime, cardamom, and turmeric used in rice dishes?
Which Indonesian spice paste, consisting of shrimp paste, shallots, garlic, chillies, and lemongrass, is the base of many dishes?
What is 'lutefisk', a traditional Scandinavian dish?
hard.a: Dried whitefish (usually cod) soaked in lye to rehydrate it

hard.q: What Brazilian Amazonian ingredient — a dark purple berry from a palm tree — is the base of the açaí bowl?
What is 'smen', an aged fermented butter used in Moroccan and North African cooking?
hard.a: Aged, salted, fermented clarified butter (similar to ghee but pungent)

hard.q: What Sicilian dish of sweet-and-sour vegetables including capers, olives, and aubergine is served at room temperature?
Which Vietnamese fermented pork sausage wrapped in banana leaves is a Tet festival specialty?
What is the name of the Georgian cheese bread boat filled with egg and butter?
What French pastry technique uses many layers of dough and butter through repeated folding to create flaky layers?
What is 'torta ahogada', a street food specialty of which Mexican city?
hard.a: Guadalajara — a pork sandwich drowned in spicy tomato salsa

hard.q: Which South Korean rice cake dish, sliced and served in gochujang sauce, is one of the most popular street foods?
What is 'Mafé' sometimes called in English, and what nut is the sauce based on?
What is 'chakapuli', the Georgian national dish traditionally eaten at Easter?
hard.a: Lamb stew with wild tarragon and sour plums (tkemali)

hard.q: What rare Sardinian cheese, illegal in many countries due to containing live insect larvae, is considered a delicacy?
What Mexican pre-Hispanic drink made from fermented agave sap predates tequila by centuries?
What is the name of the black Japanese sesame paste sweet (wagashi) made with glutinous rice?
What Egyptian street food is a thick fava bean and chickpea stew, considered the national dish?
What Scandinavian open-faced sandwich tradition, served on dense rye bread with elaborate toppings, is most associated with Denmark?
What is the key fermenting agent that gives natto its stringy texture and strong smell?
What is 'espelette', frequently used in Basque cuisine?
hard.a: A mild red pepper (piment d'Espelette) grown in the French Basque region

hard.q: What Trinidadian street food consists of hollowed-out fried dough filled with curried chickpeas?
What is 'knafeh' (also spelled kunafa), a popular Levantine dessert?
hard.a: Shredded phyllo or semolina dough filled with cheese or cream, soaked in sugar syrup

hard.q: What Chinese condiment made from fermented broad beans and chillies is called 'the soul of Sichuan cooking'?
What is 'pilpelchuma', a spicy North African Jewish condiment similar to harissa?
hard.a: A hot garlic and caraway chilli paste from Libyan Jewish cuisine

hard.q: Which Himalayan country's cuisine features 'ema datshi', a stew of chillies and cheese considered the national dish?
What is 'jollof rice' and which two West African countries most famously claim to have the superior version?
hard.a: A one-pot spiced tomato rice dish; Ghana and Nigeria rivalry is most famous

hard.q: What fermented black bean paste gives Sichuan mapo tofu its deep umami and numbing quality alongside peppercorns?
What is 'guanciale', an ingredient crucial to authentic carbonara?
hard.a: Cured pork cheek (not pancetta or bacon)

hard.q: What is the Indonesian and Malay term for a festive cone-shaped yellow rice dish served at celebrations?
What is 'calabaza en tacha', the traditional Mexican candied pumpkin served on Día de los Muertos?
hard.a: Pumpkin simmered in piloncillo syrup with cinnamon and orange peel

hard.q: What Armenian flatbread, one of the world's oldest breads, is baked on a convex clay griddle called a 'saj'?
What is 'pica pau', a Portuguese bar snack of pickled mustard beef?
hard.a: Sliced beef marinated in mustard, pickled vegetables, and spices

hard.q: Which Caribbean island is famous for 'oil down', a one-pot national dish of breadfruit, callaloo, and salted meat in coconut milk?
What is 'Makroud', a North African semolina pastry filled with dates and fried or baked?
hard.a: A Tunisian (also Algerian / Moroccan) date-filled semolina cookie soaked in honey

hard.q: What is the Sicilian ice-cream sandwich made in a soft brioche bun, eaten for breakfast?
What is 'muhamarra', a roasted red pepper and walnut dip originating in which Syrian city?
What Japanese preparation of sliced raw fish (without rice) is distinct from sushi and served with soy and wasabi?
What is the traditional spice used to colour and flavour 'chole bhature', a popular North Indian street dish?
hard.a: Amchur (dried mango powder) and chaat masala

hard.q: What is 'papadzules', a pre-Columbian Mexican dish of tortillas filled with hard-boiled eggs and drenched in a pumpkin seed sauce?
hard.a: A Yucatecan dish often called the 'enchiladas before enchiladas'

hard.q: What is 'moin moin', a steamed Nigerian dish made from blended black-eyed peas, peppers, and onions?
hard.a: A savoury bean pudding steamed in leaves or cups
